# 📁 Assignment 1 — Basic Linux Commands

> **`FileManager.sh`** — A Bash utility to manage files and directories using basic Linux commands only.
> ⚠️ `sed` command is **NOT** allowed.

---

## 📋 Table of Contents

- [Part 1 — Directory Management](#part-1--directory-management)
- [Part 2 — File Management](#part-2--file-management)
- [FileManager.sh Script](#filemanagersh-script)
- [Linux Commands Reference](#linux-commands-reference)

---

## Part 1 — Directory Management

---

### ➕ `addDir` — Create a Directory

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh addDir /tmp dir1
./FileManager.sh addDir /tmp dir2
./FileManager.sh addDir /tmp dir3
```

**Snapshot:**

![addDir](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 📂 `listDirs` — List Only Directories

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh listDirs /tmp
```

**Snapshot:**

![listDirs](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 📄 `listFiles` — List Only Files

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh listFiles /tmp
```

**Snapshot:**

![listFiles](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 📋 `listAll` — List All Contents

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh listAll /tmp
```

**Snapshot:**

![listAll](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 🗑️ `deleteDir` — Delete a Directory

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh deleteDir /tmp dir3
```

**Snapshot:**

![deleteDir](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Part 2 — File Management

---

### ➕ `addFile` — Create a File

**Command:**
```bash
# Empty file
./FileManager.sh addFile /tmp/dir1 file1.txt

# File with initial content
./FileManager.sh addFile /tmp/dir1 file1.txt "Initial Content"
```

**Snapshot:**

![addFile](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### ✏️ `addContentToFile` & `addContentToFileBegining` — Add Content

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh addContentToFile /tmp/dir1 file1.txt "Additional Content"
./FileManager.sh addContentToFileBegining /tmp/dir1 file1.txt "Prepended Content"
```

**Snapshot:**

![addContent](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 👁️ `showFileBeginingContent` & `showFileEndContent` — Show File Content

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh showFileBeginingContent /tmp/dir1 file1.txt 5
./FileManager.sh showFileEndContent /tmp/dir1 file1.txt 2
```

**Snapshot:**

![showContent](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 🔍 `showFileContentAtLine` & `showFileContentForLineRange` — Show Specific Lines

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh showFileContentAtLine /tmp/dir1 file1.txt 2
./FileManager.sh showFileContentForLineRange /tmp/dir1 file1.txt 1 3
```

**Snapshot:**

![showLineRange](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 🚚 `moveFile` — Move / Rename File

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh moveFile /tmp/dir1/file1.txt /tmp/dir1/file2.txt
./FileManager.sh moveFile /tmp/dir1/file2.txt /tmp/dir2/
```

**Snapshot:**

![moveFile](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 📋 `copyFile` — Copy File

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh copyFile /tmp/dir2/file2.txt /tmp/dir1/
./FileManager.sh copyFile /tmp/dir1/file2.txt /tmp/dir1/file3.txt
```

**Snapshot:**

![copyFile](data:image/png;base64,)

---

### 🧹 `clearFileContent` & `deleteFile` — Clear & Delete

**Command:**
```bash
./FileManager.sh clearFileContent /tmp/dir1 file3.txt
./FileManager.sh deleteFile /tmp/dir1 file2.txt
```

**Snapshot:**

![clearDelete](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## FileManager.sh Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash

COMMAND=$1

case $COMMAND in

  # ── DIRECTORY OPERATIONS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  addDir)
    mkdir -p "$2/$3"
    echo "Directory '$2/$3' created successfully." ;;

  deleteDir)
    rm -rf "$2/$3"
    echo "Directory '$2/$3' deleted successfully." ;;

  listFiles)
    echo "Files in '$2':"
    find "$2" -maxdepth 1 -type f -exec basename {} \; ;;

  listDirs)
    echo "Directories in '$2':"
    find "$2" -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -path "$2" -exec basename {} \; ;;

  listAll)
    echo "All contents in '$2':"; ls "$2" ;;

  # ── FILE OPERATIONS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  addFile)
    if [ -z "$4" ]; then
      touch "$2/$3"
      echo "File '$2/$3' created successfully (empty)."
    else
      echo "$4" > "$2/$3"
      echo "File '$2/$3' created with content: \"$4\""
    fi ;;

  addContentToFile)
    echo "$4" >> "$2/$3"
    echo "Content appended to '$2/$3'." ;;

  addContentToFileBegining)
    echo "$4" | cat - "$2/$3" > /tmp/tmpfile && mv /tmp/tmpfile "$2/$3"
    echo "Content added to beginning of '$2/$3'." ;;

  showFileBeginingContent)
    echo "Top $4 lines of '$2/$3':"; head -n "$4" "$2/$3" ;;

  showFileEndContent)
    echo "Last $4 lines of '$2/$3':"; tail -n "$4" "$2/$3" ;;

  showFileContentAtLine)
    echo "Line $4 of '$2/$3':"; awk "NR==$4" "$2/$3" ;;

  showFileContentForLineRange)
    echo "Lines $4 to $5 of '$2/$3':"; awk "NR>=$4 && NR<=$5" "$2/$3" ;;

  moveFile)
    mv "$2" "$3"; echo "File moved: '$2' -> '$3'" ;;

  copyFile)
    cp "$2" "$3"; echo "File copied: '$2' -> '$3'" ;;

  clearFileContent)
    > "$2/$3"; echo "Content of '$2/$3' cleared successfully." ;;

  deleteFile)
    rm "$2/$3"; echo "File '$2/$3' deleted successfully." ;;

  *)
    echo "Unknown command: $COMMAND" ;;
esac
```

---

## Linux Commands Reference

| Operation | Linux Command |
|---|---|
| Create directory | `mkdir -p` |
| Delete directory | `rm -rf` |
| List files only | `find -maxdepth 1 -type f` |
| List dirs only | `find -maxdepth 1 -type d` |
| List all | `ls` |
| Create file | `touch` |
| Write to file | `echo > file` |
| Append to file | `echo >> file` |
| Prepend to file | `echo \| cat - file > tmp && mv` |
| Top N lines | `head -n` |
| Last N lines | `tail -n` |
| Specific line | `awk 'NR==N'` |
| Line range | `awk 'NR>=S && NR<=E'` |
| Move / Rename | `mv` |
| Copy | `cp` |
| Clear content | `> file` |
| Delete file | `rm` |

> ⚠️ `sed` is **NOT** used anywhere in this script.

---
*Assignment 1 — Basic Linux Commands*
